message:
feat(layout,svg,parser): add XY chart layout engine, SVG renderer, and parser improvements

Add complete XY chart support across the rendering pipeline:

layout: Add LayoutAlgorithm::XyChart with full layout engine that
computes axis positions, gridlines, bar groups, and line/area series
from IrXyChartMeta. Wire into auto-selection, fallback candidates,
and cost estimation. Support categorical x-axis with bar grouping
and numeric y-axis with automatic domain computation.

render-svg: Add render_xychart_svg() producing SVG with gridlines,
axis labels, bar series (with palette rotation and rounded corners),
line series (with polyline paths and data-point circles), and area
series (with gradient fills and stroke outlines). Add axis tick
formatting and plot bounds calculation.

parser: Expand DOT parser with improved subgraph handling. Expand
Mermaid parser coverage. Add XyChart to DiagramType routing.

core: Add IrXyChartMeta and IrXySeriesKind types. Update font metrics.

cli: Wire XY chart diagram type. Add integration tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-6qd.3","title":"Prepare CLI binary distribution with GitHub releases","description":"Create dist.yml GitHub Actions workflow (modeled on DCG/FrankenTUI patterns) that builds fm-cli for all platforms: Linux x86_64, Linux aarch64, macOS Intel, macOS Apple Silicon, Windows x86_64.  {"id":"bd-6qd.5","title":"Create end-to-end release canary workflow for CLI and WASM artifacts","description":"Add release-canary smoke validation spanning CLI, WASM package, and web demo artifacts before publish.

Scope:
1. Build pre-release canary workflow that installs release artifacts in clean environments.
2. Run smoke commands for fm-cli parse/detect/render with sample corpus.
3. Run WASM package import/render checks in a minimal web project.
4. Validate web demo build/start/render path and capture screenshots/logs.
5. Publish a signed canary report artifact and fail release on unmet checks.

Why this improves the system:
- Catches packaging/distribution regressions before public release.

-{"id":"bd-6qxv","title":"XyChart: Implement dedicated layout and multi-series rendering","description":"# XyChart Full Implementation

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
xychart-beta
  title "Sales Revenue"
  x-axis [jan, feb, mar, apr, may, jun]
  y-axis "Revenue (in $)" 4000 --> 11000
  bar [5000, 6000, 7500, 8200, 9500, 10500]
  line [5000, 6000, 7000, 8000, 9000, 10000]
\`\`\`
- X-axis with categorical labels or numeric ranges
- Y-axis with label and min→max range
- Multiple series types: bar, line (on same chart)
- Chart title
- Auto-scaling to data range
- Grid lines

## Current State
- XyChart is marked UNSUPPORTED in the support table
- Parser does parse x-axis labels and series values (line/bar/area)
- No dedicated layout algorithm (falls through to Sugiyama which is wrong)
- No dedicated SVG rendering (no axes, no bars, no lines)

## Implementation Plan
1. **Layout**: Add LayoutAlgorithm::XyChart variant. Compute chart area, axis positions, bar widths, line point positions from data values and axis ranges.
2. **SVG Renderer**: Draw axes with labels and tick marks. Draw bars as filled rectangles. Draw lines as <polyline> or <path>. Draw grid lines. Add title. Apply series colors from theme.
3. **Parser**: Ensure y-axis range parsing works (min --> max syntax). Parse title.
4. **Tests**: Bar chart, line chart, mixed bar+line, custom y-axis range, multiple x-axis labels.

## This moves XyChart from UNSUPPORTED to IMPLEMENTED.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.165005953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.474798819Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","xychart"],"comments":[{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-6qxv","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:43Z"},{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-6qxv","author":"ubuntu","text":"INFRA NOTE: Unlike sequence/gantt which have separate infra beads, XyChart infra (IrXyChartMeta) should be built WITHIN this task because:
1. XyChart is simpler than sequence/gantt (no fragments, no dates)
2. The metadata is straightforward: title, x_labels, y_range, series_data
3. Creating a separate infra bead would over-fragment the work

Types to add to fm-core:
- IrXyChartMeta { title: Option<String>, x_axis: XyAxis, y_axis: XyAxis, series: Vec<XySeries> }
- XyAxis { label: Option<String>, categories: Vec<String>, min: Option<f32>, max: Option<f32> }
- XySeries { kind: XySeriesKind, values: Vec<f32> }
- XySeriesKind enum: Bar, Line, Area
- Add xy_chart_meta: Option<IrXyChartMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Bar chart with 6 categories → verify 6 bars with correct heights
2. Line chart with 6 data points → verify polyline connecting all points
3. Mixed bar+line → verify bars AND line render on same axes
4. Custom y-axis range 4000→11000 → verify axis labels and data scaling
5. Title → verify centered text above chart
6. Single data point → verify single bar or dot renders
7. Empty series → verify no crash, empty chart area
8. 100 data points → verify performance and readability","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6zd9","title":"Implement IrSequenceMeta for sequence diagram advanced features","description":"# Sequence Diagram IR Metadata

## Purpose
All advanced sequence diagram features (activation, notes, fragments, autonumber, participant groups) need structured metadata that doesn't fit in the generic node/edge model. This task adds a sequence-specific metadata structure to the IR.

## Types to Add
1. IrSequenceMeta struct containing:
   - autonumber: bool
   - activations: Vec<IrActivation> { participant: IrNodeId, start_edge: usize, end_edge: usize, depth: usize }
   - notes: Vec<IrSequenceNote> { position: NotePosition, participants: Vec<IrNodeId>, text: String }
   - fragments: Vec<IrSequenceFragment> { kind: FragmentKind, label: String, start_edge: usize, end_edge: usize, alternatives: Vec<FragmentAlternative>, children: Vec<usize> }
   - participant_groups: Vec<IrParticipantGroup> { label: String, color: Option<String>, participants: Vec<IrNodeId> }
   - lifecycle_events: Vec<IrLifecycleEvent> { kind: Create|Destroy, participant: IrNodeId, at_edge: usize }
2. NotePosition enum: LeftOf, RightOf, Over
3. FragmentKind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect
4. Add sequence_meta: Option<IrSequenceMeta> to MermaidDiagramIr

 {"id":"bd-75ja","title":"Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection","description":"## Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection

Use quotient filters (a cache-friendly alternative to Bloom filters) to detect duplicate edges during graph construction, enabling O(1) amortized duplicate detection with minimal memory overhead.

## Mathematical Foundation
A quotient filter stores a set of fingerprints compactly using quotienting: for hash h(x), split into quotient q and remainder r where h(x) = q * 2^r_bits + r. Store remainder r in slot q of a compact array, using linear probing for collisions. Properties:
- Space: ~10-25% less than Bloom filters at the same false positive rate
- Cache behavior: elements stored in contiguous sorted runs → excellent locality
- False positive rate: 2^{-r_bits} (configurable via remainder size)
- No false negatives: if element was inserted, it will be found

**For duplicate edge detection:**
When parsing a Mermaid diagram, each edge (u, v) must be checked for duplicates. For n edges:
- Naive HashSet: O(n) space, O(1) per lookup, but HashSet overhead per entry
- Quotient filter: O(n * r_bits / 8) bytes, O(1) per lookup, much lower constant factor
- For r_bits = 16: false positive rate = 1/65536, space = 2 bytes per edge
- For 10000 edges: quotient filter = ~20KB vs HashSet = ~400KB (20x savings)

**Practical consideration:** The space savings are significant for WASM target where memory is constrained. For native target, the HashSet overhead is negligible. This is primarily a WASM optimization.

## Key Papers
- Bender et al., "Don't Thrash: How to Cache Your Hash on Flash" (VLDB 2012) — quotient filter
- Pandey et al., "A General-Purpose Counting Filter" (SIGMOD 2017) — counting quotient filter
- Fan et al., "Cuckoo Filter: Practically Better Than Bloom" (CoNEXT 2014) — alternative approach

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Implement `QuotientFilter<H>` generic over hash function.
2. Use FxHash for fingerprint computation (fast, non-cryptographic).
3. Implement `insert(edge_hash)` and `may_contain(edge_hash)` operations.
4. On `may_contain() == true`: verify with exact edge comparison (handle false positives).
5. Replace `HashSet<(NodeId, NodeId)>` in parser with `QuotientFilter` for WASM target.
6. Keep `HashSet` for native target (simpler, negligible space overhead).
7. Benchmark: memory usage and parse time for 1000, 10000, 100000 edge graphs on WASM.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] QuotientFilter implementation with configurable false positive rate
- [ ] Duplicate edge detection correct (no false negatives, false positives handled)
- [ ] Memory reduction >= 10x vs HashSet for 10000-edge graphs
- [ ] Parse time comparable to HashSet (within 10%)
- [ ] WASM-specific optimization (cfg-gated)
- [ ] Property test: no false negatives over 100000 random edge sets
- [ ] Decision contract: adopt for WASM if memory reduction > 5x","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:46:30.205093497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.655567581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","parser","probabilistic","quotient-filter"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.655486068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:52.362224276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-77gh","title":"Implement IrStyleDef and IrInlineStyle types in fm-core for styling support","description":"# Styling Infrastructure in fm-core

## Purpose
Multiple diagram types need styling support (classDef, style, linkStyle directives). This task creates the shared IR types that all styling features depend on.

## Types to Add
1. IrStyleDef { name: String, properties: BTreeMap<String, String> } — for classDef definitions
2. IrInlineStyle(BTreeMap<String, String>) — for style/linkStyle directives
3. Add style_defs: Vec<IrStyleDef> to MermaidDiagramIr
4. Add inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle> to IrNode
5. Add inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle> to IrEdge

## SVG Property Mapping
Document the mapping from CSS-like property names to SVG attributes:
- fill → rect/path fill attribute
- stroke → rect/path stroke attribute
- stroke-width → stroke-width attribute
- stroke-dasharray → stroke-dasharray
- color → text fill attribute
- font-size → font-size
- font-weight → font-weight

## Security
- Sanitize all property values to prevent SVG injection
- Reject url() values, javascript:, and event handlers

## This unblocks: classDef task, style/linkStyle task, and any diagram-specific styling","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:40.857345936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39.317066072Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","infrastructure","parity"],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-77gh","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

@@ -414,4 +414,4 @@
 {"id":"bd-y6o8","title":"Implement style and linkStyle directive parsing and rendering","description":"# style / linkStyle Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Inline styling for specific nodes and edges:
\`\`\`
style nodeId fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
linkStyle 0 stroke:#ff3,stroke-width:4px,color:red
linkStyle default stroke:#333
\`\`\`

style applies to a node by ID. linkStyle applies to an edge by index (0-based declaration order) or 'default' for all edges.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes style/linkStyle lines but does not capture the property map
- No rendering path for inline styles

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`style nodeId prop:value,...\` and store as IrNodeStyle on the IrNode. Parse \`linkStyle index prop:value,...\` and store as IrEdgeStyle on the IrEdge.
2. **IR**: Add \`inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle>\` to IrNode and IrEdge. IrInlineStyle is a BTreeMap<String, String>.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Apply inline_style as SVG attributes (fill → rect fill, stroke → rect stroke, etc.). For edges, apply to <path> elements.
4. **Tests**: Verify style is applied in SVG output, verify linkStyle by index, verify default linkStyle.

## Considerations
- Property names must be mapped from CSS-like (stroke-width) to SVG attributes
- Sanitize values to prevent XSS in SVG output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.486402415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23.800287285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:23.246993348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-y6o8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-yh4v","title":"Implement gantt date parsing, task types, and duration computation","description":"# Gantt Date/Duration/Task Type Parsing

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
gantt
  dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
  title Project Plan
  excludes weekends
  section Phase 1
  Design           :done, des1, 2024-01-01, 30d
  Implementation   :active, impl1, after des1, 60d
  Testing          :crit, test1, after impl1, 2024-05-01
  Milestone        :milestone, m1, after test1, 0d
\`\`\`
- dateFormat: YYYY-MM-DD, DD/MM/YYYY, etc.
- Task types: done, active, crit (critical), milestone
- Duration: Nd (days), after taskId (dependency), absolute dates
- excludes: weekends, specific dates

## Current State
- Parser recognizes sections and task lines, but does NOT extract dates, types, durations, or dependencies
- Layout has a gantt algorithm but operates on generic nodes, not time-aware bars

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: After the colon in task lines, parse comma-separated metadata: type (done/active/crit/milestone), id, start (date or 'after id'), end (date or duration). Parse dateFormat directive. Parse excludes.
2. **IR**: Add IrGanttMeta { date_format, excludes, sections: Vec<IrGanttSection> } with IrGanttTask { id, name, task_type, start, end, depends_on }.
3. **Layout**: Compute actual x-positions from dates. Apply excludes to skip weekends. Position milestone as diamonds.
4. **Tests**: Date formats, durations, after-dependencies, excludes, milestones, crit tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.294100500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31.708821785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gantt","parity","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","depends_on_id":"bd-0bu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:28.205838996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"bd-yh4v","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL GANTT DIRECTIVES TO PARSE:
- todayMarker stroke-width:5px,stroke:#0f0,opacity:0.5 → custom styling for the today line. Store as optional style string in IrGanttMeta.
- tickInterval 1day / 1week / 1month → controls axis label granularity. Store as enum (Day/Week/Month/Quarter/Year) in IrGanttMeta.
- inclusiveEndDates → when true, task end dates include the end day. Store as bool in IrGanttMeta.
- weekday monday → sets start of week. Store as optional u8 in IrGanttMeta.
- axisFormat %Y-%m-%d → date format for axis labels. Store as String in IrGanttMeta.

These must ALL be parsed for full parity. Update the IrGanttMeta type definition in bd-0bu2 accordingly.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zmbp","title":"State: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# State Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Basic states and transitions work (~75% parser). Missing all UML pseudostate types that make state diagrams expressive.

## Gaps
1. Start/end pseudo-state special rendering ([*])
2. Fork/join parallel bars
3. Choice pseudo-state (diamond shape)
4. History states (H / H*)
5. Composite/nested states with proper containment
6. Notes on states
7. Entry/exit actions on transitions
8. Guard conditions [guard] on transitions
9. State descriptions (state name : description)
10. Concurrency regions (-- separator within composite state)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/stateDiagram.html","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:19.858400163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.981662394Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-2gwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:48.302936595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-qj2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.980550820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zmbp","depends_on_id":"bd-zn9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:49.128081199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-zn9n","title":"Implement composite/nested states with containment and concurrency","description":"# Composite/Nested States and Concurrency

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active {
    [*] --> Processing
    Processing --> Done
    --
    [*] --> Monitoring
    Monitoring --> Alert
  }
  Idle --> Active
  Active --> Idle
\`\`\`
- States can contain child states (composite state)
- \`--\` separator creates concurrent regions within a composite state
- Each concurrent region has independent start/end states
- Nesting can be arbitrarily deep

## Current State
- Composite state syntax (\`state Name { ... }\`) IS parsed — creates subgraphs
- Concurrency separator \`--\` is NOT parsed
- Layout does not enforce visual containment of child states
- SVG renderer draws clusters but doesn't render concurrency dividers

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`--\` within composite state blocks as region separators. Each region becomes a separate sub-cluster within the parent cluster.
2. **Layout**: Ensure cluster bounds fully contain all child node bounds. For concurrent regions, split the cluster horizontally with a dashed divider.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw composite states with rounded rectangle borders. Draw dashed horizontal line for concurrency dividers. Add region labels if present.
4. **Tests**: Nested states, three levels deep, concurrent regions, transitions crossing region boundaries.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.201073543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:30:25.099192208Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","parser","render","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zn9n","depends_on_id":"bd-2gwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:31.377491237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":153,"issue_id":"bd-zn9n","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)
